MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT VALERIE LOUIE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF BILL CHENG'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (TRANSACTION ID # 61238237) FILED BY DEFENDANT LOUIE, VALERIE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VALERIE LOUIE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BY PLAINTIFF BILL CHENG IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. BILL CHENG SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY AGAINST MS. LOUIE. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, BILL CHENG ALLEGES THAT MS. LOUIE ALLOWED HER DOG TO BE IN A LOCATION WITHOUT ANY FORM OF RESTRAINT OR CONTROL OF A COMPETENT PERSON TO VICIOUSLY ATTACK BILL CHENG EVEN THOUGH MS. LOUIE KNEW THAT HER DOG HAD UNUSUALLY VIOLENT PROPENSITIES AND HAD ONLY EIGHT DAYS PREVIOUSLY BITTEN MARK CHENG CAUSING INJURIES TO MARK CHENG. THESE FACTS, IF PROVED, ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A FINDING THAT MS. LOUIE ENGAGED IN "DESPICABLE CONDUCT WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE RIGHTS OR SAFETY OF OTHERS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CIVIL CODE 3294(C)(1). THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANT, VALERIE LOUIE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the request for punitive damages by plaintiff Bill Cheng in the first amended complaint is DENIED. Bill Cheng sufficiently alleges ultimate facts supporting punitive damages liability against Ms. Louie. Liberally construed, Bill Cheng alleges that Ms. Louie allowed her dog to be in a location without any form of restraint or control of a competent person to viciously attack Bill Cheng even though Ms. Louie knew that her dog had unusually violent propensities and had only eight days previously bitten Mark Cheng causing injuries to Mark Cheng. These facts, if proved, are sufficient to support a finding that Ms. Louie engaged in "despicable conduct with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others" within the meaning of Civil Code 3294(c)(1). The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported. (L&M302)
